title: Time Tracking - Column Summary
excerpt: Previously, I used to be able to see the summary of my Time Tracking column but the summary row is now grayed out and I don’t have the option in settings to Show Column Summary. Is this a feature they recently did away w…

posts.author: McKennaOMG (McKenna Dorame)
posts.cookedHtml: Previously, I used to be able to see the summary of my Time Tracking column but the summary row is now grayed out and I don’t have the option in settings to Show Column Summary. Is this a feature they recently did away with?

posts.author: Patnard (Patrick Chenard)
posts.cookedHtml: I was looking for the same thing. I was really useful and I am wondering if it’s a bug.

posts.author: GiannisKoukounas (Giannis Koukounas)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @Patnard and @McKennaOMG !

This is indeed a feature that has been deprecated.

Many monday users have also been asking this question, and I know that this issue has been raised with monday’s internal team.

It was intended to make working with the boards more efficient, but clearly it has not been having the intended impact.

I will make sure to let you know should I have any new information!

Best Regards,
Giannis, Implementation Consultant at thespelas.com

posts.author: Valeriia.H (Valeriia)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @Patnard and @McKennaOMG !

Our team released Time in Status add-on which helps to track time the issue has spent in each workflow status easily. So you can monitor how fast the issue moves between statuses.

You’ll get user-friendly data on the board in a few seconds.

You can try it on the Monday Marketplace and share your experience with us.
Hope you find this add-on helpful.

posts.author: jarodthornton (Jarod Thornton)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey There,

We now have to export to see time tracking totals I can no longer see a summary of time being spent at a glance and it’s a pain to manage now.

Please bring this back!!!

posts.author: GiannisKoukounas (Giannis Koukounas)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @jarodthornton .

For better or for worse, I am not a part of monday’s decision making team.

We have communicated however together with other monday partners that this is a useful feature that should be brought back, and I am hopeful that it eventually will.

I will let you know should I learn anything new.

Best Regards,
Giannis, Implementation Consultant at thespelas.com

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Jarod - we hear where you’re coming from and appreciate your feedback here To combat this change, you can instead utilise the Formula Column to calculate that column summary. Let me know if you have any issues!

posts.author: Patnard (Patrick Chenard)
posts.cookedHtml: If I can chip in, a useful part of that feature was that it would give a summary for the groups as well, since they get their summary. Do we know if this is a bug, or an intentionally removed feature for an ulterior motive?

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: This feature was removed due to increased feedback regarding its calculation and reporting performance - which was significantly impacting workflows. You’re welcome to send me a private message to share more of your feedback

posts.author: gweb73 (Guy Long)
posts.cookedHtml:
I guess there must be a good reason for removing this feature, but on the face of it, it seems like adding a formula column to do the calculation would also impact performance. We already had the option to hide the column summary so that would have been a way to avoid the overhead if desired.
I used this feature and it’s sudden removal without warning is quite frustrating.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: I hear you Guy and understand that the change was unexpected. I have shared your comments directly with our team internally and apologise for the inconvenience this has caused!

posts.author: Cosmin (Clipa)
posts.cookedHtml:
For sure Time Tracking column summary should be back! This was such an essential feature for project management!

I can understand that we can use the Formula Column, but it’s not really the same thing…

Looking forward to seeing it back!

posts.author: Tgreene (Taylor Greene)
posts.cookedHtml:
With this feature being discontinued, how would I best obtain a summary of time spent in a group as shown below?

time tracking860×338 23.6 KB

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Taylor!

You can pull this information using a formula column:

It’s important to note, that in order for this to work, the time-tracking will need to be paused

For any feedback, you are welcome to submit your comments through this form → Time tracking footer - feedback

posts.author: Rach12 (Rachael )
posts.cookedHtml: How do you use the formula column for this - thank you!

posts.author: Rach12 (Rachael )
posts.cookedHtml: Hi, what is the formula? Also is there a formula to only show work days for time tracking?

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @Rach12

To set this up, input the time tracking column into the formula column, and choose the duration value based on what you’d like to track - i.e. in seconds, minutes or hours:

As for tracking hours per workday, I have followed up with my team internally and will circle back here once i get an answer

posts.author: Rach12 (Rachael )
posts.cookedHtml: Hi the formula provided shows a large value (formula column in snip) - doesn’t work. I found this formula on a thread that shows the amount of days ROUNDDOWN({Entire Product#Hours}/24,0) (Total Days in snip) however, I need to remove the weekends. Please let me know what you find out! Thank you!

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have recently heard back from our team internally and I am afraid at this time, there is no way to differentiate between the - two workdays/weekends - since when we call on the time tracking column in the formula, we cannot see the date-timestamps, just the amount of time recorded. I apologise for the setback here

Is the issue that team members are not stopping the time-tracking after their working week?

posts.author: Rach12 (Rachael )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Bianca - do you know if we can 1. Only have time tracking 8 hours at a time? Then after 40 hours past it will stop until 48 hours past? or 2. Is there a formula to subtract 16 hours per day?

The issue is once a status changes it continues to run afterhours or weekends.
@JCorrell do you have any experience with formulas and time tracking too?

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thanks for getting back to me!

Hm, if you’d like to subtract x amount of hours from each day, could something like this work for you?


Screen Shot 2023-07-21 at 10.18.56 am824×414 20.5 KB

Let me know

posts.author: Rach12 (Rachael )
posts.cookedHtml:
What I ended up doing was making a date column for end date then using the creation log and date column to show workdays -1,

WORKDAYS({Stop Date},{Creation Log})-1

then used the summary of the column set to average to see the average turnaround time of products. Looks like this is the best solution at the time. Thank you.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: This is a great workaround Rachael Glad to see you were able to come up with something here!

posts.author: CaitlinEpperson (Caitlin Epperson)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey this is really problematic and time inefficient to not be able to easy and quickly see a summary of the time tracked and it makes the column a little pointless. In addition to not seeing the summary in the main groups, we can’t see the summary in subtasks either. This makes invoicing very time consuming for those of us needing to track actual hours and not estimates. This was feature that made me switch to Monday.com over other options so I could have all of my time tracking with the projects. Will there be any attempt to remedy this in the future?

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Caitlin,

I am really sorry to hear of your frustration here - I understand that especially for your use-case that this is a significant setback for you. Whilst I can guarantee a return of this feature in the near future, I can certainly share these comments with the team internally. We do ask that you submit this feedback via the dedicated form, as these comments go directly to the specific product team in-charge

posts.author: Enzo (Enzo Ferreira)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi all,

I found a workaround to count hours and minutes between 2 datetime columns, I haven’t seen it yet in any forums so I’ll share it with you.

I tried using the timetracking column but it’s to restrictive to do anything with it, here is what I’ve done :

I have 2 datetime columns :

I will use those columns to determine opened hours, for my case I’m working from 9am to 6pm

Then you need 2 date time columns you want to count working hours from :

When you’re columns are set, create a formula column and paste the following code in :


IF(SUM(IF(AND(HOUR({Creation log}) > HOUR({Begin})-1,
	   HOUR({Creation log}) < HOUR({End}),
       MINUTE({Creation log}) > 0),
	60-MINUTE({Creation log}),
	0),
	IF(AND(HOUR({End date})-8 > 0,HOUR({End date})-8 < 12),
	MINUTE({End date}),
	0)) > 59,
    IF(SUM(IF(AND(HOUR({Creation log}) > HOUR({Begin})-1,
	   HOUR({Creation log}) < HOUR({End}),
       MINUTE({Creation log}) > 0),
	60-MINUTE({Creation log}),
	0),
	IF(AND(HOUR({End date})-8 > 0,HOUR({End date})-8 < 12),
	MINUTE({End date}),
	0)) = 0,
        CONCATENATE(SUM(WORKDAYS({End date},{Creation log})*MINUS(HOUR({End}),HOUR({Begin}))-(MINUS(HOUR({End}),HOUR({Begin}))*2),
    IF(WORKDAYS({Creation log},{Creation log}) > 0,
    IF(HOUR({Creation log}) < HOUR({Begin}),
		MINUS(HOUR({End}),HOUR({Begin})),
		IF(HOUR({Creation log}) < HOUR({End}),
        	IF(MINUTE({Creation log}) = 0,
              MINUS(MINUS(HOUR({End}),
                          HOUR({Begin})),
                    MINUS(HOUR({Creation log}),
                          HOUR({Begin}))),
                        
              MINUS(MINUS(HOUR({End}),
                          HOUR({Begin})),
                    MINUS(HOUR({Creation log}),
                          HOUR({Begin})))-1),
            0
		)
	),
	0
),
    IF(WORKDAYS({End date},{End date}) > 0,
    IF(HOUR({End date}) < HOUR({Begin}),
		0,
		IF(HOUR({End date}) < HOUR({End}),
			HOUR({End date})-HOUR({Begin}),
            MINUS(HOUR({End}),HOUR({Begin}))
		)
	),
	0
))+1,
                    "h0",
                    MOD(SUM(IF(AND(HOUR({Creation log}) > HOUR({Begin})-1,
	   HOUR({Creation log}) < HOUR({End}),
       MINUTE({Creation log}) > 0),
	60-MINUTE({Creation log}),
	0),
	IF(AND(HOUR({End date})-8 > 0,HOUR({End date})-8 < 12),
	MINUTE({End date}),
	0)),60),
                    "min"),
                    
        CONCATENATE(SUM(WORKDAYS({End date},{Creation log})*MINUS(HOUR({End}),HOUR({Begin}))-(MINUS(HOUR({End}),HOUR({Begin}))*2),
    IF(WORKDAYS({Creation log},{Creation log}) > 0,
    IF(HOUR({Creation log}) < HOUR({Begin}),
		MINUS(HOUR({End}),HOUR({Begin})),
		IF(HOUR({Creation log}) < HOUR({End}),
        	IF(MINUTE({Creation log}) = 0,
              MINUS(MINUS(HOUR({End}),
                          HOUR({Begin})),
                    MINUS(HOUR({Creation log}),
                          HOUR({Begin}))),
                        
              MINUS(MINUS(HOUR({End}),
                          HOUR({Begin})),
                    MINUS(HOUR({Creation log}),
                          HOUR({Begin})))-1),
            0
		)
	),
	0
),
    IF(WORKDAYS({End date},{End date}) > 0,
    IF(HOUR({End date}) < HOUR({Begin}),
		0,
		IF(HOUR({End date}) < HOUR({End}),
			HOUR({End date})-HOUR({Begin}),
            MINUS(HOUR({End}),HOUR({Begin}))
		)
	),
	0
))+1,
                    "h",
                    MOD(SUM(IF(AND(HOUR({Creation log}) > HOUR({Begin})-1,
	   HOUR({Creation log}) < HOUR({End}),
       MINUTE({Creation log}) > 0),
	60-MINUTE({Creation log}),
	0),
	IF(AND(HOUR({End date})-8 > 0,HOUR({End date})-8 < 12),
	MINUTE({End date}),
	0)),60)
                    ,"min")
    ),
    IF(SUM(IF(AND(HOUR({Creation log}) > HOUR({Begin})-1,
	   HOUR({Creation log}) < HOUR({End}),
       MINUTE({Creation log}) > 0),
	60-MINUTE({Creation log}),
	0),
	IF(AND(HOUR({End date})-8 > 0,HOUR({End date})-8 < 12),
	MINUTE({End date}),
	0)) = 0,
        CONCATENATE(SUM(WORKDAYS({End date},{Creation log})*MINUS(HOUR({End}),HOUR({Begin}))-(MINUS(HOUR({End}),HOUR({Begin}))*2),
    IF(WORKDAYS({Creation log},{Creation log}) > 0,
    IF(HOUR({Creation log}) < HOUR({Begin}),
		MINUS(HOUR({End}),HOUR({Begin})),
		IF(HOUR({Creation log}) < HOUR({End}),
        	IF(MINUTE({Creation log}) = 0,
              MINUS(MINUS(HOUR({End}),
                          HOUR({Begin})),
                    MINUS(HOUR({Creation log}),
                          HOUR({Begin}))),
                        
              MINUS(MINUS(HOUR({End}),
                          HOUR({Begin})),
                    MINUS(HOUR({Creation log}),
                          HOUR({Begin})))-1),
            0
		)
	),
	0
),
    IF(WORKDAYS({End date},{End date}) > 0,
    IF(HOUR({End date}) < HOUR({Begin}),
		0,
		IF(HOUR({End date}) < HOUR({End}),
			HOUR({End date})-HOUR({Begin}),
            MINUS(HOUR({End}),HOUR({Begin}))
		)
	),
	0
)),
                    "h0",
                    SUM(IF(AND(HOUR({Creation log}) > HOUR({Begin})-1,
	   HOUR({Creation log}) < HOUR({End}),
       MINUTE({Creation log}) > 0),
	60-MINUTE({Creation log}),
	0),
	IF(AND(HOUR({End date})-8 > 0,HOUR({End date})-8 < 12),
	MINUTE({End date}),
	0)),
                    "min"),
                    
        CONCATENATE(SUM(WORKDAYS({End date},{Creation log})*MINUS(HOUR({End}),HOUR({Begin}))-(MINUS(HOUR({End}),HOUR({Begin}))*2),
    IF(WORKDAYS({Creation log},{Creation log}) > 0,
    IF(HOUR({Creation log}) < HOUR({Begin}),
		MINUS(HOUR({End}),HOUR({Begin})),
		IF(HOUR({Creation log}) < HOUR({End}),
        	IF(MINUTE({Creation log}) = 0,
              MINUS(MINUS(HOUR({End}),
                          HOUR({Begin})),
                    MINUS(HOUR({Creation log}),
                          HOUR({Begin}))),
                        
              MINUS(MINUS(HOUR({End}),
                          HOUR({Begin})),
                    MINUS(HOUR({Creation log}),
                          HOUR({Begin})))-1),
            0
		)
	),
	0
),
    IF(WORKDAYS({End date},{End date}) > 0,
    IF(HOUR({End date}) < HOUR({Begin}),
		0,
		IF(HOUR({End date}) < HOUR({End}),
			HOUR({End date})-HOUR({Begin}),
            MINUS(HOUR({End}),HOUR({Begin}))
		)
	),
	0
)),
                    "h",
                    SUM(IF(AND(HOUR({Creation log}) > HOUR({Begin})-1,
	   HOUR({Creation log}) < HOUR({End}),
       MINUTE({Creation log}) > 0),
	60-MINUTE({Creation log}),
	0),
	IF(AND(HOUR({End date})-8 > 0,HOUR({End date})-8 < 12),
	MINUTE({End date}),
	0))
                    ,"min")
    )
)

If your columns names are different from the one I use, fell free to take this code into a code editor, and Ctrl+F each references of the 4 columns and modify them with yours.

This will count hours between the “Begin” and “End” columns only for days that are setup in your working week on Monday.com (On top right click on your profile photo → My profile → “Account schedules”)

If you want more details on how it works to modify it, feel free to add a reply on this topic and I’ll answer whenever I see it.

posts.author: Valeriia.H (Valeriia)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone!
Have you ever faced the problem of tracking the team effectiveness? It’s a popular request from Monday users. I would like to share a great news with you to make the workflow process even more convenient

Our team has presented Time in Status add-on which helps to track time the item has spent in each workflow status easily. So you can monitor how fast the item moves between statuses. Great, isn’t it?

You’ll get user-friendly data on the board in a few seconds.

You can try it on the Monday Marketplace and share your experience with us.
Hope you find this add-on helpful.

posts.author: Valeriia.H (Valeriia)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there

I am happy to announced that we realised Time in Status app which track time the Item has spent in each workflow status. So you can monitor how fast the Item moves between statuses using app with reports.
You’ll get user-friendly data on the board in a few seconds.

Знімок екрана 2024-01-26 о 03.33.481920×488 50.2 KB

Also you will be able to track how much time your issue spends in each status and much more by generating 3 types of reports:

  • Time in Status Report
  • Status Count Report
  • Transition Count Report

And use charts to visualize your reports a sample of a certain number according to time range/item range, or a single item:

  • Pie Charts
  • Bar Charts
  • Area Charts

The app is also available in the format of board integration.

Знімок екрана 2024-01-26 о 03.35.011538×634 95.8 KB

You can try it on the Monday Marketplace and share your experience with us.
Hope you find this app helpful.

posts.author: wendy_aem (Wendy Lim)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @BiancaT - I tried using the {PM Hours#Hour} formula to get the summation for total number of hours. However, the results seem to be multiplied.
e.g 30min = 1800 (looks like they are taking the data * 60)
Is there something wrong with how i am applying the formula?

Screenshot 2024-08-09 at 5.39.12 PM602×586 21.8 KB

posts.author: davidjentjens (David Maisonnette Jentjens)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone,

I totally understand the frustration around trying to automate SLA timers within Monday, especially for those of you who have specific needs, like starting and stopping timers during business hours. After spending quite some time working through the Monday API, I’ve managed to build a reliable solution that automates this process without needing constant manual input.

The system I’ve developed ensures that:

• Timers start automatically at the beginning of the workday for items with specific SLA conditions (e.g., Business Hours).

• At the end of the day, timers are automatically updated, and tasks are properly tracked based on the SLA status.

It’s built to work seamlessly with Monday’s boards, even handling different workflows without you needing to worry about keeping track of everything manually.

If this sounds like the solution you’ve been looking for, I’d be happy to chat and implement it for you. It’s a tailored solution that I can customize to fit your specific use case, ensuring the automation works exactly how you need it.

Feel free to DM me if you’re interested in learning more or if you’d like a quick demo!

Also, if you want to chat via email, you can send one to sharplyapps@gmail.com, I will be answering on the same day.
